title: Sangram article manifest (editorial + i18n contract, series slot C4)
description: Machine-readable half of the Sangram editorial contract: one manifest per article, the single source of truth for the article's locales, layers, stable example IDs, canonical-script example texts, loci, translations and provenance. Prose half: sangram/editorial/SANGRAM_EDITORIAL_I18N_CONTRACT.mdx.

contract_version.description: Semver of the C4 editorial contract this manifest conforms to.

canonical_script.description: The one canonical storage encoding for all Sanskrit text in the manifest. IAST (default rendering) and Devanagari (user preference) are derived at render time by the canonical sanskrit-util transform; hand-written second copies are banned.

id.description: Stable article ID (art:<slug>). The ID grammar is fixed here; ID assignment and the article network live in the C2 TOC. IDs are never reused.

toc_ref.description: Reference into the C2 article-network TOC node that owns this article; null until C2 assigns it (C2 interface point).

thesis_refs.description: Optional links to internal programme theses, same format as the atlas contract (B1).

layers.description: Which of the two content layers the article provides. Most core articles provide both.

en.description: The EN locale is a translation of a settled RU revision and never leads RU (charter risk R6, wave-W2 gate). translated_from must name the date of an entry in article.revisions.

revisions.description: Append-only publication history (charter editorial pipeline). Empty while the article is unpublished; pipeline states like 'draft' are volatile and never stored.

translated_from.description: Date of the RU revision this translation mirrors.

slp1Text.description: SLP1 (ASCII) only: letters, avagraha ('), accent marks (/ \\ ^), danda (|), space, nasal tilde, and light punctuation. Devanagari or IAST diacritics here violate the single-canonical-copy rule.

id.description: Stable example ID (ex:<article-slug>:<n>). Never reused; retiring an example leaves a gap and a retraction note, it never frees the number.

segmentation_slp1.description: Optional sandhi-split form, same canonical encoding.

corpus.description: Corpus slug. The closed registry of corpora with quality/rights/liveness gates is owned by the C3 evidence method (C3 interface point); 'constructed' marks a pedagogical example with no corpus witness.

evidence.description: Same evidence rule as the B1 atlas contract: public evidence must carry an https URL; internal evidence is named but never addressed (no URL).
